rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical sulfonylation of olefins with sodium sulfinates to access vinyl, alkyl, and allyl sulfones using NaI as redox mediator
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of iodide to I2 (and/or direct oxidation of sulfinate) generates sulfonyl radical A; cathode reduces protons to H2 and, for activated olefins, reduces the C-centered radical B to a carbanion

key_intermediates.name: C-centered carbon radical B (β-sulfonyl alkyl radical after addition to olefin)
β-sulfonyl carbanion D (Path II, after SET reduction of B)
I2 generated by anodic oxidation of I-

electron_flow: At the anode, iodide is oxidized to I2 which oxidizes ArSO2Na to sulfonyl radical A (direct anodic oxidation of sulfinate also possible); A adds to the olefin giving C-radical B. Path I: B is trapped by I2 to give β-iodo sulfone C, which undergoes base-promoted HI elimination to the vinyl sulfone. Path II (EWG/cyclohexenyl olefins): B is reduced at the cathode to anion D, which is reoxidized by I2 or protonated/eliminated to give alkyl or allyl sulfone; protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Addition of 4.0 equiv TEMPO fully suppressed formation of vinyl sulfone 1b, supporting a radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: N,N-diallyl-4-methylbenzenesulfonamide (radical cyclization probe)
probe_reagents.observation: Afforded cyclized pyrrolidine product 5d (iodomethyl-tosylmethyl pyrrolidine) in 35%, indicating a radical addition/cyclization process

probe_reagents.name: Standard reaction (isolation of iodinated intermediate)
probe_reagents.observation: β-iodo sulfone byproduct 1f isolated in 15% under standard conditions, supporting iodosulfone C as intermediate of Path I

notes: Two mechanistic pathways are proposed depending on olefin electronics: Path I (favored for simple/aliphatic olefins) goes through β-iodo sulfone C with base-mediated HI elimination to give vinyl sulfone; Path II operates for electron-poor olefins (EWG) or cyclohexene-type substrates where cathodic SET reduces radical B to a carbanion giving alkyl or allyl sulfones. The reaction also proceeds in absence of NaI (lower yield), indicating direct anodic oxidation of sulfinate is a viable parallel route to radical A.
